hero.badge: NucleoHub unlocks your future career journey
hero.title: Nuclear medicine: When atoms help save lives
hero.description: Explore careers, knowledge, and scholarship opportunities through a modern visual experience designed for Vietnamese students.

matchmaker.title: AI Career Matchmaker (Mockup)
matchmaker.description: What do you enjoy? Pick your interest to see a career suggestion.

code.title: Suggested: AI Engineer in Healthcare
code.detail: You are a strong fit for building AI models for PET/SPECT image reading, optimizing hospital workflows, and creating clinical decision assistants.

medical.title: Suggested: Nuclear Medicine Physician
medical.detail: You can follow a medical pathway focused on radionuclide-based diagnosis and therapy, combining clinical practice with advanced technology.

machine.title: Suggested: Nuclear Medical Equipment Engineer
machine.detail: You may thrive in designing and operating PET/CT and SPECT/CT systems, as well as quality assurance and radiation safety.

income.title: High income potential
income.detail: Demand for highly skilled talent is rising in hospitals and healthtech companies.

social.title: Social impact
social.detail: You help detect diseases earlier and improve treatment outcomes for many patients.

tech.title: Cutting-edge technology
tech.detail: This field combines AI, digital simulation, Digital Twin models, and advanced imaging systems.

timeline.title: Roadmap from HUST to the world
timeline.description: A sample path for students going from technical foundations to international opportunities.

items.desc: Build a strong base in Math, Physics, Programming, and academic English.
Work on AI healthcare projects, medical imaging pipelines, detector simulation, and lab practice.
Apply to programs in Germany/Russia and broaden your research in Medical Physics.
Work in research institutes, high-tech hospitals, or healthtech startups.

game.title: NucleoHub Games
game.intro: Clear 3 science puzzles to unlock your trainee badge in an 8-bit pixel game vibe.
game.levelHint: Level 2 and 3 are temporarily locked for balanced progression.
game.wrongReveal: Too bad, radiation got you! GAME OVER
game.victoryDesc: You cleared all 3 obstacles and reached the finish flag.
game.gameOverDesc: Too bad, radiation got you! GAME OVER

questions.title: The Glowing Banana Case
questions.prompt: Did you know that eating a banana gives your body a tiny amount of natural radiation? What happens if you eat 100 bananas at once?

options.text: You will glow in the dark.
Nothing scary; the exposure is still very small.
You will break all electronic devices nearby.

questions.explain: Great choice. Natural radiation exists around us all the time. In nuclear medicine, tracer doses are carefully controlled: enough for imaging signals, not for dramatic movie effects.

questions.title: The Doctor’s Magic Lens
questions.prompt: In a nuclear medicine room (SPECT/PET), what is the scanner’s main role?

options.text: It blasts strong rays into the body to burn unhealthy cells immediately.
It captures bones exactly like a regular X-ray.
It records signals emitted from the tracer inside the body.
It measures skin temperature to predict disease.

questions.explain: Surprise. Unlike X-ray imaging, the signal source here is inside the body. PET/SPECT acts like a functional lens, revealing how tissues and organs are working.

questions.title: Temporary Superpower
questions.prompt: After receiving a tracer injection for nuclear medicine imaging, which temporary superpower does your body gain?

options.text: Emitting tiny signals that scanners can detect to build functional images.
Turning your body into a metal magnet.
Becoming completely immune to all diseases.

questions.explain: Exactly. For a short time, your body becomes a tiny lighthouse emitting measurable signals. That data helps clinicians evaluate internal biological activity.

wiki.title: Knowledge Wiki
wiki.description: A foundational library on isotopes, diagnostic techniques, and clinical applications.

items.useCase: PET/CT for oncology and neurology diagnostics
items.note: Widely used in FDG-PET to observe glucose metabolism.

items.useCase: SPECT for cardiology, bone, and renal imaging
items.note: A common isotope due to its favorable diagnostic properties.
Supports both diagnosis and targeted radionuclide therapy.

career.title: Community & Career Guidance
career.description: A space to share learning experiences, skill roadmaps, and internship opportunities.

university.title: University & Scholarship Guidance
university.description: A sample list you can grow into a personalized university recommendation module.

items.name: Hanoi University of Science and Technology (HUST)
items.focus: Biomedical engineering, medical electronics, and AI in healthcare
items.scholarshipHint: Merit scholarships and student research funds.

items.name: Technical University of Munich (TUM)
items.focus: Medical Physics, Imaging Science, and health data
items.scholarshipHint: DAAD and multiple international research funding tracks.

items.name: NRNU MEPhI
items.focus: Nuclear Engineering, Radiochemistry, and Detector Physics
items.scholarshipHint: Government scholarships and bilateral partnership programs.

footer.title: NucleoHub: Future Navigation Station
footer.description: A career guidance and deep-learning platform on Nuclear Medicine for high school and university students in Vietnam.
